'''Mukuda no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (''umakuta no kuni no miyatsuko'', ''makuta no kuni no miyatsuko'', ''umakuta kokuzō'', ''makuta kokuzō'') was a {{ill|kuni no miyatsuko|ja|国造}} who ruled the central-western part of {{ill|Kazusa Province|ja|上総国}}.

== Overview ==

=== Ancestry ===
*The "Kokuzō Hongi" of the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'' states that the line begins when, during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇|lt=Emperor Seimu|lt_ja=成務朝}}, '''Fukakaoomi-no-Mikoto''', son of {{ill|Takekoro-no-Mikoto|ja|建許呂命}} — descendant of {{ill|Amatsuhikone|ja|天津彦根命}} and ancestor of the {{ill|Ibaraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|茨城国造}} — was appointed kuni no miyatsuko.

=== Clan ===
'''{{ill|Kashiwade-no-Ōtomobe clan|ja|膳大伴部氏}}''' (''kashiwade-no-ōtomobe-uji''; {{ill|kabane|ja|カバネ|lt=kabane|lt_ja=姓}} unknown).

== Base ==

=== Territory ===
The kuni no miyatsuko's territory was the region then called '''Mukuda Province''', corresponding to the later '''{{ill|Mōda District|ja|望陀郡}}''' of {{ill|Kazusa Province|ja|上総国}} — all of present-day {{ill|Sodegaura|ja|袖ケ浦市}}, most of {{ill|Kisarazu|ja|木更津市}}, and parts of {{ill|Kimitsu, Chiba|ja|君津市}} and {{ill|Kamogawa, Chiba|ja|鴨川市}}. The {{ill|Gion-Nagasuga Kofun Cluster|ja|祇園・長須賀古墳群}} in Kisarazu in the lower {{ill|Obitsu River|ja|小櫃川}} basin, including the {{ill|Kinreizuka Kofun|ja|金鈴塚古墳}}, is thought to have been built by the Mukuda no Kuni no Miyatsuko.

== Tutelary Shrine ==
Unknown.

=== Tombs ===
*'''{{ill|Iigozuka Kofun|ja|飯籠塚古墳}}''' (''iigozuka kofun'')
*: A 102-m-long {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳}} in {{ill|Kimitsu, Chiba|ja|君津市|lt=Kimitsu City|lt_ja=君津市}} in {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, thought to have been built in the {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}.
*'''{{ill|Sengen Shrine Kofun|ja|箕輪浅間神社古墳|lt=Sengen Shrine Kofun|lt_ja=浅間神社古墳}}''' (''sengen jinja kofun'')
*: A 105-m-long keyhole-shaped kofun in Kimitsu City, Chiba Prefecture, thought to have been built in the 4th century.
*'''{{ill|Hakusan Shrine Kofun (Kimitsu)|ja|白山神社古墳 (君津市)|lt=Hakusan Shrine Kofun|lt_ja=白山神社古墳}}''' (''hakusan jinja kofun'')
*: An 89-m-long keyhole-shaped kofun in Kimitsu City, Chiba Prefecture, thought to have been built in the 4th century.
*'''{{ill|Gion-Nagasuga Kofun Cluster|ja|祇園・長須賀古墳群}}''' (''gion-nagasuga kofungun'')
*: A kofun cluster in {{ill|Kisarazu|ja|木更津市}} in Chiba Prefecture, built from the middle of the {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} to the {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}}.

== See also ==
*{{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}}

== References ==
*''Kokushi Daijiten'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan)
*Sakamoto Tarō, Hirano Kunio, ''Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan)
*''Nihon-shi Kō-jiten'' (Yamakawa Shuppansha)
*''Shintō Daijiten'' (Rinsen Shoten)

== External links ==
* [http://www.haniwakan.com/kenkyu/boso/makuta.html Walking the Kofun of Bōsō: Kofun of Mukuda Province]

{{DEFAULTSORT:うまくたのくにのみやつこ}}
[[Category:日本の歴史関連のスタブ項目]]
[[Category:上総国]]
[[Category:千葉県の歴史]]
[[Category:Kuni no miyatsuko]]
[[ja:馬来田国造]]
[[Category:Need translation]]
